label: Standard — 5 beneficiaries, 1 shared house
description: The original scenario: five beneficiaries, one house jointly owned by three of them.

label: Two shared houses — 6 beneficiaries
description: Six beneficiaries split across two separate jointly-owned houses, each needing all its owners included.

label: Large family — 8 beneficiaries, 1 shared house
description: Eight beneficiaries with smaller individual shares, showing how combining several is needed to reach the minimum.

label: Small family — 3 beneficiaries, 1 shared house
description: Just three beneficiaries, two of whom jointly own a smaller house.

label: Complex family — 10 beneficiaries, 3 overlapping houses
description: Ten beneficiaries and three houses whose ownership overlaps — three people each co-own more than one house.
